goal: Create cleaner, more diverse content chunks using MMS forced alignment instead of weak uniform timing, then compare against the tuned chunked baseline.
reason: The clean alignment pilot proves the data path works and improves over weak timing, but it does not beat the tuned chunked baseline yet.
interpretation: Weak uniform timing failed because the audio slices often did not match the target text.
MMS forced alignment produced clean Arabic character timings for 37/40 diverse pilot rows, with exact romanized-to-Arabic projection for all 37 successful rows.
Partial initialization is important when expanding the content vocabulary: shared encoder weights and shared character rows are reused, while new characters are learned from the new aligned chunks.
The pilot is too small to replace the current content baseline, but it is the correct route for leaving the fixed chunk phrase list.
next_step: Scale the MMS forced-alignment corpus from 40 pilot rows to a larger diverse set, then retrain with partial initialization and evaluate against the same tuned chunked baseline.
